text.title: AI-Powered PM Tools (PM-Agile) — Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules every AI-PM-tools selection or rollout must enforce. The rules pin the discipline that converts vendor-feature dazzle into a justified, integration-tested, audit-traceable platform choice.

rule.statement: Tool selection produces a decision record with: incumbent platform, AI-tier price delta (% over base seat), integration fit score (SSO + audit + connectors), ROI gate threshold, and a named buyer's signature. Selections without all five fields are rejected.
rule.rationale: Selections without traceable rationale drift in renewal cycles. A signed decision record makes the choice defensible at renewal and at audit.

rule.statement: Evaluate the incumbent platform's AI tier first; switching to a different vendor requires the incumbent uplift to fail the ROI gate by ≥20%. Cross-vendor switches at less than 20% margin are rejected because the switching cost dominates the AI delta.
rule.rationale: Switching costs (data migration, retraining, integration rewiring) typically eat 12-18 months of any uplift gain. The 20% rule prevents AI-feature FOMO from triggering avoidable churn.

rule.statement: The integration inventory (SSO provider, audit-log sink, connectors to Git, calendar, comms) MUST be enumerated and confirmed against the vendor's published support matrix BEFORE contract signature. "Coming soon" connectors are treated as absent.
rule.rationale: Integration gaps surface 3-6 months post-purchase when the rollout stalls. Forcing pre-signature confirmation prevents the "we'll write a custom integration" expense the vendor evaluation hid.

rule.statement: AI features that mutate the PM source of truth (auto-close, auto-assign, auto-status-change) are disabled by default at rollout and re-enabled per feature only after a 30-day shadow run logs no incorrect mutations.

rationale.source: Site-reliability principle of gradual rollout; LLM-Ops practice

rule.rationale: Vendor AI features marketed as "smart automation" frequently produce wrong mutations at edge cases (renamed projects, custom statuses). Shadow runs surface this without corrupting live data.

rule.statement: Per-seat AI uplift cost is divided by measured weekly time saved (hours/seat) collected over a 60-day pilot. Selections where the cost-per-hour-saved exceeds the team's blended hourly rate are rejected; the vendor offer must beat that gate.
rule.rationale: Marketing-claimed uplift rarely survives a measured pilot. Forcing a cost-per-hour-saved gate prevents paying for a vendor's AI brand premium that does not materialise in throughput.

example.title: Decision record with all fields
example.code: incumbent: Linear ai_tier_delta: +35%/seat integration_fit_score: 4/4 (SSO, audit, Git, calendar) roi_gate_threshold: cost-per-hour-saved < $45 (blended rate) pilot_result: $32/h saved → passes gate buyer: vpe:ruslan decision: enable AI tier; shadow-run all auto-mutations for 30d.
example.note: All five fields populated. r1, r3, r4 satisfied; r5 verified by pilot.

example.title: Brand-driven switch
example.code: incumbent: Linear (works, no complaints) proposed: Jira+Rovo reason: "Rovo's agents look impressive in demo" roi_analysis: (none)
example.note: Violates r1 (no decision record), r2 (no incumbent evaluation), r5 (no pilot). Reject.
